general_info.definition: Metal-free fluorescent quaternized Carbon dots with bacterial contact-enhanced fluorescence emission for bacterial imaging and selective antibacterial activity.

source: quaternary ammonium-carrying organo silane (dimethyloctadecyl[3-(trimethoxysilyl)propy]ammonium chloride, abbreviated as Si-QAC)(0.5,1.0, or 2.0mL)

post_treatment: Dialysis against deionized water using 1 kDa MWCO membrane for 2 days

structural.morphology: varying size distribution with spherical nanoparticles

applications.performance: Selectively lights up Gram-positive bacteria with multi-color fluorescence after culturing with CDs for 1h. Fluorescence intensity increases upon when bound with Gram-positive bacteria cells. No significant change in fluorescence emission for Gram-negative bacteria.
applications.comparative_advantage: rapid and simple alternative than traditional Gram staining; provides rapid and accurate imaging-guided identification and counting of Gram-positive bacteria.

applications.domain: In vivo antibacterial therapy - S. aureus infected mice
applications.performance: CD treatment resulted in 98.1% bacterial reduction within 2.5h at 5µg/mL
applications.comparative_advantage: CDs are a promising antibacterial agent with low toxicity to mammalian cells. Such a selective cell killing property of the cationic CDs. CDs have the advantages of low-cost and facile synthesis, excellent biocompatibility, and self-traceable capability.

feedback.label: Bad
